top_signals.title: Iran Tightens Grip on Strait of Hormuz Tanker Traffic
top_signals.summary: Just when the oil market was getting comfortable with the idea that the Strait of Hormuz was reopening, Iran reminded everyone who's still directing traffic. Iran's joint military command warned on Thursday that all oil tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz must follow routes approved by Tehran or face an "immediate and forceful response." The warning, carried by Iranian state television, also cautioned that any U.S. interference in the waterway would prompt a "rapid and decisive reaction." The warning came a day after U.S. and Iranian negotiators…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil Rises After Selloff With Flows Through Hormuz in Focus
top_signals.summary: Oil rebounded in thin trading following a sharp selloff, with traders weighing the outlook of increased supply through the Strait of Hormuz and continuing talks between the US and Iran.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Asset Purchase Facility: Gilt Sales – Market Notice 19 June 2026
top_signals.summary: This Market Notice sets out the schedule for sales in Q3 2026 of gilts held in the Asset Purchase Facility (APF) for monetary policy purposes.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Institutional Bitcoin Adoption Explained: How Blackrock, Fidelity and Others Embraced BTC
top_signals.summary: Institutional adoption is when organizations such as asset managers, corporations, hedge funds, banks, pension funds, and insurers get involved in crypto. This can mean investing in bitcoin directly or building products and services around it. The trend accelerated sharply after spot bitcoin ETFs launched in January 2024 and now spans most categories of regulated institutional […]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, April 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income decreased less than $0.1 billion (less than 0.1 percent at a monthly rate) in April, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-decreased $19.9 billion (0.1 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $111.1 billion (0.5 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $114.0 billion in April. Personal saving was $611.7 billion in April, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of DPI-was 2.6 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
